what_it_does: Alternative AI coding runtimes. Codex (OpenAI) is a cloud sandbox runtime. OpenCode is an open-source terminal AI assistant. Hermes is a tool-use focused model/runtime.
operational_patterns: Codex: cloud-sandboxed execution with git integration
Codex: async task submission and polling for results
OpenCode: terminal-native with provider-agnostic model routing
Hermes: tool-use optimized execution with structured outputs
runtime_patterns: Codex: sandboxed container per task, no local filesystem access
Codex: async execution — submit task, poll for completion
OpenCode: local execution with streaming output
OpenCode: provider abstraction (OpenAI, Anthropic, local models)
Hermes: structured tool calling with validation
governance_patterns: Codex: sandbox isolation prevents destructive actions
Codex: approval-based deployment (human reviews before merge)
ux_patterns: OpenCode: terminal UI with real-time streaming
Hermes: structured JSON output for tool results
execution_loops: Codex: submit → sandbox execute → review → merge
reusable_primitives: Async task submission with polling (Codex pattern)
redundancy_risk: MEDIUM — UMH model_router already does provider-agnostic routing. RuntimeGraph does capability-based selection. Sandbox isolation is a genuine gap for Beast node execution.
adaptation_priority: MEDIUM — async submission pattern and sandbox isolation are useful for the distributed organism. Provider-agnostic routing is already solved.

umh_mapping.async_execution: substrate/organism/coordinator.py — execute_objective() is synchronous; async pattern is a gap
umh_mapping.sandbox_isolation: nodes/environments/ — work_packet.py has EnvironmentType but no sandbox enforcement yet

eos_relevance: LOW — EOS agents don't need sandbox isolation
trinity_relevance: LOW — products run in their own containers
expected_leverage_gain: 0.20 — async task submission and sandbox isolation are genuine capability gaps for distributed execution
